entry.title: DHH22 Hackathon Project
entry.summary: Project Background The project is a topic of Helsinki Digital Humanities Hackathon 2022.The theme is Network of Parlamint. The data include national parliament speeches of 17 european countries. We eventually selected and focus on the Spanish term 12, UK term 57, Slovenian term 7 datasets. Research question How is the speeches of MPs and mentions of them are related to the power? Is there...

entry.title: Eigenvalue, singular value & SVD
entry.summary: Eigenvalue & Eigenvector Definition: $A x = \lambda x$ => $|A - \lambda E|=0$ The $\lambda$ holds the equation is eigenvalue and the $x$ is eigenvector. $A$ should be a square matrix. If $A(n,n)$ is not invertible ($A$ is singular, rank is less than n), there must be at least one $\lambda$ is zero. For example, now square matrix $ A=\left[ \begin{matrix} 12 & 8 ...

entry.title: Chinese word segmentation statistical approach(I)
entry.summary: Language model is a model predicting probabilities of sentences or sequences of words. This blog discuss about how to do word segmentation. Unlike European languages, some Asian languages like Chinese and Janpanese, don’t have spaces between each words. Humans understand languages because we automatically have recognized words in heads. For machinese, those are merely sequences of symbols and ...

entry.title: Chinese word segmentation statistical approach(II)
entry.summary: Introduction This blog introduces the bigram model for word segmentation. So the bigram model considers previous character which makes more sense in terms of context. The equation should be: \(P(W_{i}|W_{i-1})=\frac{C(W_{i},W_{i-1})}{C(W_{i-1})}\) However, we may also wonder what if $W_{i-1}$ is zero? The probability cannot be computed in this case. The combination of $W_{i}$ and $W_{i-1}$ can ...

entry.title: Parsing mathematical expressions with tree structure

link.title: Parsing mathematical expressions with tree structure

entry.summary: Credit to Problem solving with algorithms and data structures using Python section 7.5 & 7.6 & 7.7 The idea is to use binary tree to parse mathematical tree. For example, (3*(2+5)) the tree should be like: First we need to have a class for binary tree. # some may be modified class BinaryTree: def __init__(self,rootobj): self.key=rootobj self.leftchild=None ...
